title: dalbergia boehmii subsp. stuhlmannii
intro: Dalbergia boehmii subsp. stuhlmannii, also known as African blackwood, is a subspecies of tree native to the tropical regions of East Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with African blackwood include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support wound healing and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's heartwood is often used to make a tea or infused in oil to create a medicinal tincture, which can be applied topically to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
